## Metrics

The federation-health instrument set served by `GET /metrics`. Names and label keys are
constants in `metrics::names` — **this table mirrors that module; change them together.**
Counters are constructed *without* the `_total` suffix (the Prometheus exporter appends it;
a unit test in `metrics.rs` pins the rendered names). All instruments record through the
typed `AppState.metrics` handle; when `[telemetry] metrics_enabled = false` the same handle
drops measurements at the SDK boundary, so call sites never branch.

| Rendered name | Type | Labels | Recorded by |
|---|---|---|---|
| `http_requests_total` | counter | `route` (matched template or `unmatched`), `status_class` | `metrics::http_metrics_middleware` (outside the rate limiter → 429s count; inside CORS → preflights don't) |
| `firehose_subscribers` | gauge | — | `SubscriberGuard` in `sync_subscribe_repos.rs` (+1 connect, −1 on any exit path) |
| `firehose_events_total` | counter | `frame` (`commit`/`account`/`identity`/`sync`) | `Firehose::broadcast` — the single fan-out choke point |
| `firehose_backfill_window_seconds` | gauge | — | `firehose_gc.rs` after each sweep (age of `MIN(sequenced_at)`; 0 when the log is empty) |
| `firehose_gc_swept_total` / `firehose_gc_last_run_timestamp` | counter / gauge | — | `firehose_gc.rs` after each completed pass |
| `labeler_watch_changes_total` / `labeler_watch_last_run_timestamp` | counter / gauge | — | `labeler_watch.rs` after each completed pass (`changes` = label rows upserted + removed; error early-returns skip the timestamp on purpose — staleness is the alarm) |
| `blob_gc_swept_total` / `blob_gc_last_run_timestamp` | counter / gauge | — | `blob_gc.rs` after each completed pass (a failed-to-start pass leaves the timestamp stale on purpose — that staleness is the alarm) |
| `blob_mirror_synced_total` / `blob_mirror_last_run_timestamp` | counter / gauge | — | `blob_mirror/mod.rs` after each completed pass (`synced` = bucket uploads + deletes; a failed-to-enumerate pass leaves the timestamp stale on purpose — that staleness is the alarm) |
| `blob_scrub_flagged_total` / `blob_scrub_healed_total` / `blob_scrub_last_run_timestamp` | counter / counter / gauge | — | `blob_scrub.rs` after each completed pass (`flagged` = unhealed integrity problems this pass — missing files, hash/size mismatches, orphan files; `healed` = bad/missing files repaired from the blob-mirror bucket; a failed-to-list pass leaves the timestamp stale on purpose). `routes/get_blob.rs` also adds to `flagged` when a serve-time re-hash catches a corrupt file — same alarm channel, so an operator watches one counter |
| `account_reaper_swept_total` / `account_reaper_last_run_timestamp` | counter / gauge | — | `account_reaper.rs` after each completed pass |
| `agent_claim_sweep_swept_total` / `agent_claim_sweep_last_run_timestamp` | counter / gauge | — | `agent_claim_sweep.rs` after each completed pass (error early-returns skip the timestamp on purpose — staleness is the alarm) |
| `admin_nonce_sweep_swept_total` / `admin_nonce_sweep_last_run_timestamp` | counter / gauge | — | `admin_nonce_sweep.rs` after each completed pass |
| `relay_crawl_requests_total` | counter | `outcome` (`ok`/`rejected`/`exhausted`) | `crawler.rs` per finished notification |
| `proxy_requests_total` | counter | `upstream` (`appview`/`chat`/`moderation`/`header_target` — the last for an `app.bsky.*`/`chat.bsky.*` request that used an `atproto-proxy` header to override its default), `status_class` | `app.rs::xrpc_handler` on both the streaming and munged paths |
| `proxy_upstream_lag_seconds` | histogram | — | `read_after_write/mod.rs`, same conditional value as the `Atproto-Upstream-Lag` header |
| `rate_limit_rejections_total` | counter | `limiter` (`global_ip`/`endpoint_ip`/`account_writes`) | `rate_limit.rs` middleware; `record_write.rs` for write points |
| `migration_imports_total` | counter | `outcome` (`ok`/`error`) | `routes/import_repo.rs` |

Cardinality rule: label values are always drawn from small fixed sets or the route table —
never from request data (raw URIs, rkeys, DIDs, hostnames).

## Hard Rules

**Routes must not import from other routes.**
If two routes share logic, that logic belongs in `auth/` (pure) or `db/` (queries). A route
importing from another route creates hidden coupling and makes it impossible to reason about
a handler in isolation.

**Every `.rs` file with runtime behavior must have a pattern comment.**
Add `// pattern: Functional Core`, `// pattern: Imperative Shell`, or
`// pattern: Mixed (unavoidable)` at the top of any file containing functions or
orchestration logic. Files with only types, constants, or re-exports are exempt.

**`db/` submodules own queries, not transactions.**
Business-logic transactions (multi-table atomic operations) live in the route handler or
a dedicated helper called by the handler — not inside `db/` functions. `db/` functions
accept `&SqlitePool` or `&mut SqliteTransaction`; they never open transactions themselves
unless the operation is inherently single-table.

**Authentication must never be cookie-based; permissive CORS on the public surface depends on it.**
`app.rs` applies `CorsLayer::permissive()` to the public surface (landing, `.well-known`, OAuth,
agent registration, all XRPC, static assets) but deliberately **not** to the admin/provisioning
`/v1/*` surface (same-origin only). Permissive CORS is safe only because every auth path is
Bearer/DPoP/signed-request — never an ambient cookie — so a hostile origin cannot ride a logged-in
user's credentials. If any future auth mechanism becomes cookie-based, the permissive CORS layer
must be tightened (explicit origin allowlist + credentials handling) in the same change.

## Adding a New Route

1. Create `src/routes/<name>.rs` with `// pattern: Imperative Shell` at the top.
2. If the handler needs shared auth logic → add to `auth/` (pure) or use an existing extractor.
3. If the handler needs a new DB query → add to the appropriate `db/` submodule.
4. If it's an XRPC procedure: a JSON input body is parsed with `LexiconInput<T>` (never bare
   `axum::Json<T>`) after vendoring its lexicon document (`crates/pds/lexicons/README.md`); a
   no-input procedure takes `NoInputBody` instead. Both must be the handler's final extractor.
5. Register in `src/app.rs` router.
6. Add a `.bru` file in `bruno/` (see root AGENTS.md).

## Adding a New DB Query

1. Identify the owning entity (`accounts`, `oauth`, etc.).
2. Add the function to the matching `db/<entity>.rs` file.
3. If no matching file exists, create one with `// pattern: Imperative Shell`.
4. Export the function and any new data struct via `db/mod.rs` (`pub mod <entity>;`).

content.text:
Done and verified. Option B is implemented on a fresh branch (`serve-server-did-json`), all checks green.

## What changed

[did_json.rs](crates/pds/src/routes/did_json.rs) now special-cases the server's own identity: when the request host maps to `resolve_server_did()`, the handler serves a document **synthesized from config** — the reference-PDS shape (`id` + `#atproto_pds` service pointing at the public URL, no verification methods) — and never touches the accounts join. The check runs *before* the DB read, so a coincidental account row can never shadow the server identity, and a non-did:web `EZPDS_SERVER_DID` (e.g. a did:plc) simply never matches since hosts only ever map to `did:web:*`. The pure `server_did_document` builder trims a trailing slash off the public URL defensively.

This also fixes the live gap you just discovered: `obsign.org/.well-known/did.json` currently 404s (no row existed), and after this deploys it will resolve — and it will keep resolving through the `pds.obsign.org` migration with zero database work, since both the DID and the endpoint come from the same env vars you'll be flipping anyway.

## Verification

- `cargo clippy -p pds --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- All 13 did_json-area tests pass, including four new ones: document shape + slash-trim (pure), synthesized serve without an account row, config-wins-over-account-row precedence, and did:plc server DID falling through to the account path
- The pre-existing `didweb_account_migrates_onto_custos_without_custos_serving_did_json` guard still passes — migrated *user* did:web accounts remain gated on opt-in exactly as before
- Changelog fragment added ([server-did-json.fixed.md](changelog.d/server-did-json.fixed.md)); no ticket refs in source; the route path is unchanged so Bruno parity is unaffected

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Why serving a **key-less** document is correct here: atproto inter-service auth JWTs are always signed by the *account's* `#atproto` key and verified against the account's DID doc — the server DID only ever appears as an `aud` string, which needs no key resolution. So the server document's only job is existence + the `#atproto_pds` serviceEndpoint, exactly what bsky.social's own did.json contains.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

The work is uncommitted on the branch. Want me to commit and open a PR (I'd run the full `just ci-pds` gate first), or do you want to look it over first?

## Summary

`/.well-known/did.json` previously only served opted-in **account** documents through the `accounts` ⋈ `did_documents` gate, so the server's own DID (`EZPDS_SERVER_DID`, e.g. `did:web:obsign.org`) never resolved — the infrastructure identity has no accounts row, and creating a ghost one would violate the account invariants (no repo, no keys, visible to admin listing/purge tooling).

This PR special-cases the server's own identity in the route:

- When the request host maps to `resolve_server_did()`, the handler serves a document **synthesized from config** — the reference-PDS shape: `id` + an `#atproto_pds` service entry pointing at the public URL, with no verification methods (inter-service auth is always verified against per-account `#atproto` keys; the server DID only ever appears as an `aud` string).
- The branch runs **before** the account-gated DB read, so a coincidental account row can never shadow the server identity — the served document stays exactly what `describeServer` advertises, both deriving from `resolve_server_did()`.
- A non-did:web `server_did` (e.g. did:plc) never matches, since `host_to_did_web` only produces `did:web:*` strings — it falls through to the account path with no special handling.

This also makes the server DID hostname-migration-proof: repointing the deployment (public URL + server DID env flip) keeps `did.json` resolving with zero database work.

## Test plan

- New pure-function test: document shape + trailing-slash trim on the public URL.
- New route tests: synthesized serve with no account row; config-wins-over-account-row precedence; did:plc `server_did` falling through to the account gate (404 without a row).
- Pre-existing guards still green, including `didweb_account_migrates_onto_custos_without_custos_serving_did_json` — migrated user did:web accounts remain strictly opt-in.
- `cargo clippy -p pds --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt` clean; changelog fragment included.
EOF
)"
